items.title: Ganga Expressway Inauguration
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the Ganga Expressway in Hardoi, Uttar Pradesh. The expressway is expected to boost connectivity and economic growth in the region.
items.summary: The Ganga Expressway is a 594-kilometer-long expressway that will connect the cities of Meerut and Prayagraj. The project is estimated to have cost around ₹32,000 crore. The expressway is expected to reduce travel time between the two cities from 10 hours to just 5 hours.
keyPoints: Length of the expressway: 594 kilometers
Cost of the project: ₹32,000 crore
Expected reduction in travel time: 5 hours
Cities connected: Meerut and Prayagraj
importantPoints: National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) - full form, founded in 1988, headquartered in New Delhi
Related scheme: Bharatmala Pariyojana, a government scheme to develop highways in India
Historical context: The Ganga Expressway is one of the longest expressways in India, and its construction is expected to boost economic growth in the region
Policy implication: The expressway is expected to improve connectivity and reduce travel time, making it easier for people and goods to move around the region
India rank: The Ganga Expressway is one of the top 10 longest expressways in India

items.title: India's Export Target
items.whyInNews: Union Minister of Commerce and Industry Piyush Goyal chaired a review meeting to discuss the plan to achieve the export target of $2 trillion by 2030-31.
items.summary: The government has set an ambitious target of achieving $2 trillion in exports by 2030-31. The meeting discussed various strategies to achieve this target, including increasing exports of goods and services. The government also plans to increase the share of exports in the country's GDP.
keyPoints: Export target: $2 trillion
Year to achieve target: 2030-31
Share of exports in GDP: expected to increase
Type of exports: goods and services
importantPoints: Ministry of Commerce and Industry - full form, founded in 1995, headquartered in New Delhi
Related scheme: Foreign Trade Policy, a government scheme to promote exports in India
Historical context: India's exports have been growing steadily over the years, and the government aims to increase this growth
Policy implication: Achieving the export target is expected to boost economic growth and create jobs in the country
India rank: India is one of the top 10 exporters in the world

items.title: Vande Bharat Train
items.whyInNews: The Jammu Tawi-Srinagar Vande Bharat train was flagged off, marking a significant improvement in rail connectivity between the two cities.
items.summary: The Vande Bharat train is a high-speed train that will reduce travel time between Jammu and Srinagar from 10 hours to just 6.5 hours. The train has several modern features, including automatic doors and a GPS-based passenger information system. The train will run at a speed of 130 kilometers per hour.
keyPoints: Speed of the train: 130 kilometers per hour
Travel time: 6.5 hours
Features: automatic doors, GPS-based passenger information system
Route: Jammu Tawi to Srinagar
importantPoints: Indian Railways - full form, founded in 1853, headquartered in New Delhi
Related scheme: Vande Bharat Mission, a government scheme to promote rail connectivity in India
Historical context: The Vande Bharat train is one of the fastest trains in India, and its introduction is expected to boost tourism and economic growth in the region
Policy implication: The train is expected to improve connectivity and reduce travel time, making it easier for people to travel between the two cities
India rank: The Vande Bharat train is one of the top 10 fastest trains in India

items.title: India-Sri Lanka Naval Exercise
items.whyInNews: India and Sri Lanka conducted a joint naval exercise, IN-SLN DIVEKX 2026, to strengthen maritime cooperation between the two countries.
items.summary: The exercise was conducted in the Indian Ocean and involved several naval ships and aircraft from both countries. The exercise aimed to enhance interoperability and cooperation between the two navies. The exercise also included training on maritime security and counter-terrorism.
keyPoints: Name of the exercise: IN-SLN DIVEKX 2026
Location: Indian Ocean
Participating countries: India and Sri Lanka
Type of training: maritime security and counter-terrorism
importantPoints: Indian Navy - full form, founded in 1950, headquartered in New Delhi
Related scheme: Indian Ocean Naval Symposium, a government scheme to promote maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean
Historical context: India and Sri Lanka have a long history of maritime cooperation, and the exercise aims to strengthen this cooperation
Policy implication: The exercise is expected to enhance maritime security and cooperation between the two countries
India rank: India is one of the top 10 naval powers in the world

items.title: Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0
items.whyInNews: The government signed MoUs with the states of Uttarakhand, Karnataka, and Tripura to improve the implementation of the Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0.
items.summary: The Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0 aims to provide functional household tap connections to all households in rural areas by 2024. The mission also aims to improve the quality of water and reduce the burden on women and girls who collect water. The government has allocated ₹1.42 lakh crore for the mission.
keyPoints: Name of the mission: Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0
Objective: provide functional household tap connections to all households in rural areas
Allocation: ₹1.42 lakh crore
States signed MoU: Uttarakhand, Karnataka, and Tripura
importantPoints: Ministry of Jal Shakti - full form, founded in 2019, headquartered in New Delhi
Related scheme: National Water Policy, a government scheme to promote water conservation and management in India
Historical context: The Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0 is an extension of the previous mission, which aimed to provide drinking water to all households in rural areas
Policy implication: The mission is expected to improve the quality of life of people in rural areas and reduce the burden on women and girls who collect water
India rank: India is one of the top 10 countries in the world in terms of water resources

items.title: DRDO and Indian Navy Conduct Successful Missile Test
items.whyInNews: The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and the Indian Navy conducted a successful test of a naval version of the surface-to-air missile, Barak-1.
items.summary: The test was conducted from a naval ship and the missile successfully intercepted a target at a distance of 50 kilometers. The missile is designed to provide air defence to naval ships and can intercept targets at a range of up to 70 kilometers.
keyPoints: Name of the missile: Barak-1
Type of missile: surface-to-air missile
Range: up to 70 kilometers
Conducting organisations: DRDO and Indian Navy
importantPoints: Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) - full form, founded in 1958, headquartered in New Delhi
Related scheme: Make in India, a government scheme to promote indigenous defence production in India
Historical context: The Barak-1 missile is an indigenous development and has been successfully tested several times in the past
Policy implication: The successful test of the missile is expected to enhance the air defence capabilities of the Indian Navy
India rank: India is one of the top 10 countries in the world in terms of defence spending

items.title: India's Coal Gasification Project
items.whyInNews: The Ministry of Coal has launched a financial incentive scheme to promote coal gasification projects in the country.
items.summary: The scheme aims to encourage the use of coal gasification technology to produce methanol, ammonia, and other chemicals. The government has allocated ₹1,000 crore for the scheme and plans to support the development of 10 coal gasification projects in the next 5 years.
keyPoints: Name of the scheme: Coal Gasification Promotion Scheme
Objective: promote coal gasification projects in the country
Allocation: ₹1,000 crore
Number of projects to be supported: 10
importantPoints: Ministry of Coal - full form, founded in 1973, headquartered in New Delhi
Related scheme: National Clean Energy Fund, a government scheme to promote clean energy in India
Historical context: Coal gasification is a clean and efficient way to use coal and can help reduce greenhouse gas emissions
Policy implication: The scheme is expected to promote the use of coal gasification technology and reduce dependence on imported fuels
India rank: India is one of the top 5 countries in the world in terms of coal production

items.title: India's Export of Electric Vehicles
items.whyInNews: India has set a target to export 1 million electric vehicles by 2025.
items.summary: The government has launched several initiatives to promote the export of electric vehicles, including the Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Electric Vehicles (FAME) scheme. The scheme provides incentives to manufacturers to produce electric vehicles and promotes the use of electric vehicles in the country.
keyPoints: Target: export 1 million electric vehicles by 2025
Name of the scheme: FAME scheme
Objective: promote the export of electric vehicles
Type of vehicles: electric vehicles

Policy implication: The export of electric vehicles is expected to promote the growth of the automotive industry and create jobs in the country
India rank: India is one of the top 10 countries in the world in terms of electric vehicle production

items.title: India's Ranking in Global Innovation Index
items.whyInNews: India has improved its ranking in the Global Innovation Index (GII) to 40th position.
items.summary: The GII is a ranking of countries based on their innovation capabilities and performance. India has improved its ranking due to its strong performance in areas such as information and communication technology, and business sophistication.

India rank: India is one of the top 10 countries in the world in terms of innovation

items.title: India's Forest Cover
items.whyInNews: India's forest cover has increased by 2,261 square kilometers, according to the latest report by the Forest Survey of India.
items.summary: The report states that the total forest cover in the country is now 80.9 million hectares, which is 24.62% of the country's geographical area. The increase in forest cover is attributed to the efforts of the government and other stakeholders to promote afforestation and reforestation.
keyPoints: Increase in forest cover: 2,261 square kilometers
Total forest cover: 80.9 million hectares
Percentage of geographical area: 24.62%
Conducting organisation: Forest Survey of India
importantPoints: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change - full form, founded in 1985, headquartered in New Delhi
Related scheme: National Forest Policy, a government scheme to promote forest conservation and management in India
Historical context: India has been consistently increasing its forest cover over the past few years
Policy implication: The increase in forest cover is expected to help mitigate climate change and promote biodiversity
India rank: India is one of the top 10 countries in the world in terms of forest cover

items.title: Nepal Airlines Apologises for Cartographic Inaccuracies
items.whyInNews: Nepal Airlines apologised for showing Jammu and Kashmir as part of Pakistan in their network map on social media. The map was shared on X and contained significant cartographic inaccuracies regarding international boundaries.
items.summary: Nepal Airlines issued an apology for the error in their network map, stating that it did not reflect the official stance of Nepal or Nepal Airlines. The map was shared on social media platform X and sparked controversy. Nepal Airlines has since corrected the map to reflect the accurate international boundaries.
keyPoints: Nepal Airlines apologised for cartographic inaccuracies in their network map
The map was shared on social media platform X
The error did not reflect the official stance of Nepal or Nepal Airlines
The map has been corrected to reflect accurate international boundaries
importantPoints: International Air Transport Association (IATA) - full form, founded in 1945, headquartered in Montreal
Article 1 of the Constitution of India defines the territory of India
The error sparked controversy and highlighted the importance of accurate cartography
Nepal Airlines is the flag carrier of Nepal, founded in 1958
The incident has implications for India's territorial integrity and international relations
items.examRelevance: GK: International Organisations, Geography: International Boundaries

items.title: India Accelerates Maritime Expansion Plan
items.whyInNews: India has accelerated its maritime expansion plan amid concerns over the Strait of Hormuz. An inter-ministerial review meeting was chaired by Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal.
items.summary: The meeting focused on building a robust and future-ready maritime ecosystem to safeguard national supply chains. The plan aims to enhance India's maritime capabilities and reduce dependence on the Strait of Hormuz. The expansion plan will also promote economic growth and development.
keyPoints: India has accelerated its maritime expansion plan
The plan aims to enhance India's maritime capabilities
The expansion plan will promote economic growth and development
The plan will reduce dependence on the Strait of Hormuz
importantPoints: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways - founded in 2019, headquartered in New Delhi
Article 297 of the Constitution of India deals with maritime jurisdiction
The Strait of Hormuz is a critical shipping lane, with over 20% of global oil trade passing through it
India's maritime expansion plan is part of its broader strategy to enhance economic growth and development
The plan has implications for India's energy security and trade relationships
items.examRelevance: Economy: Maritime Trade, Polity: Constitutional Provisions

items.title: IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad Wins Fifth Consecutive Match
items.whyInNews: Sunrisers Hyderabad won their fifth consecutive match in the IPL 2026, defeating Mumbai Indians. The match was played at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai.
items.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad's win was led by TraviShek and Klaasen, who scored crucial runs. The match was a high-scoring affair, with both teams scoring over 200 runs. Sunrisers Hyderabad's win has taken them to the top of the points table.

The match was played at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai

items.title: Exit Polls Predict Close Contest in West Bengal
items.whyInNews: Exit polls have predicted a close contest in the West Bengal assembly elections. The polls suggest that the ruling Trinamool Congress may face a challenge from the Bharatiya Janata Party.
items.summary: The exit polls were conducted by various agencies, including Axis My India. The polls suggest that the Trinamool Congress may win around 150 seats, while the Bharatiya Janata Party may win around 120 seats. The polls also suggest that the Congress party may win around 20 seats.
keyPoints: Exit polls have predicted a close contest in West Bengal
The Trinamool Congress may win around 150 seats
The Bharatiya Janata Party may win around 120 seats
The Congress party may win around 20 seats
importantPoints: Election Commission of India (ECI) - full form, founded in 1950, headquartered in New Delhi
Article 324 of the Constitution of India deals with the election process
The West Bengal assembly elections are being held in two phases
The elections are being seen as a crucial test for the ruling Trinamool Congress
The outcome of the elections will have implications for the state's politics and governance

items.title: IMD Forecasts Thunderstorms and Rainfall in Odisha
items.whyInNews: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecasted thunderstorms and rainfall in Odisha. The forecast is valid for the next few days.
items.summary: The IMD has predicted that the thunderstorms and rainfall will bring relief from the extreme heat conditions in the state. The forecast is based on the latest satellite imagery and weather models. The IMD has advised people to take necessary precautions during the thunderstorms.
keyPoints: The IMD has forecasted thunderstorms and rainfall in Odisha
The forecast is valid for the next few days
The thunderstorms and rainfall will bring relief from the extreme heat conditions
The IMD has advised people to take necessary precautions

The forecast has implications for the state's agriculture and water resources
